# Step 13 — Antigen-Specificity and Multi-Effector Model {#step13}

Two final questions: is any effector→`r cfg$outcome_label` association **antigen-specific or broad**
(focal `r FOCAL_ANTIGEN` vs an aggregate mean across the antigens carrying that effector), and when the
three functions compete in one model, **which — if any — is independently on the path**? Reported per
arm and mode; the aggregate uses each effector's own available-antigen set.

```{r step13-run, results='asis'}
.stage(13, "antigen-specificity and multi-effector model")
if (!RUN_EFF_AGG) {
  cat("*Step 13 skipped (run_effector_aggregate = FALSE).*\n\n")
} else {
  fit1 <- function(d, pred) {
    dd <- d[stats::complete.cases(d[, c(OUTCOME, pred)]), , drop = FALSE]
    if (nrow(dd) < NETWORK_MIN) return(NULL)
    f <- lm(reformulate(pred, response = OUTCOME), data = dd); co <- summary(f)$coefficients[pred, ]
    data.frame(n = nrow(dd), R2 = round(summary(f)$r.squared, 3),
               beta = round(unname(co[1]), 3), p = signif(unname(co[4]), 3))
  }
  .modes <- if (RUN_STD_RERUNS) MODES else "raw"
  for (mode in .modes) {
    cat("## Data mode: `", mode, "`", mode_desc(mode), "\n\n", sep = "")
    wd <- wide_for(mode)
    if (!(OUTCOME %in% names(wd))) { cat("*Outcome missing.*\n\n"); next }
    # build focal + aggregate columns per effector
    agg_names <- c()
    for (fn in EFFECTORS) {
      cols <- intersect(eff_all(fn), names(wd))
      if (length(cols)) { wd[[paste0("agg_", fn)]] <- rowMeans(wd[, cols, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
                          agg_names <- c(agg_names, paste0("agg_", fn)) }
    }
    strat <- setNames(lapply(cfg$maternal_arms, function(m) dplyr::filter(wd, arm_name == m)), cfg$maternal_arms)

    # (a) focal vs aggregate, per effector
    rows <- list()
    for (fn in EFFECTORS) {
      foc <- paste0(FOCAL_ANTIGEN, "_", fn); agg <- paste0("agg_", fn)
      for (m in cfg$maternal_arms) {
        d <- strat[[m]]
        rf <- if (foc %in% names(d)) fit1(d, foc) else NULL
        ra <- if (agg %in% names(d)) fit1(d, agg) else NULL
        if (!is.null(rf)) rows[[length(rows)+1]] <- cbind(effector = fn, arm = m, scope = paste0("focal ", FOCAL_ANTIGEN), rf)
        if (!is.null(ra)) rows[[length(rows)+1]] <- cbind(effector = fn, arm = m,
          scope = paste0("aggregate (", length(intersect(eff_all(fn), names(wd))), " antigens)"), ra)
      }
    }
    tb <- do.call(rbind, rows)
    if (!is.null(tb)) print(knitr::kable(tb, row.names = FALSE,
      caption = paste0("Antigen-specificity of effector\u2192", cfg$outcome_label, " [", mode,
      "]: focal ", FOCAL_ANTIGEN, " vs aggregate score, univariate."))); cat("\n")

    # (b) joint focal three-effector model; (c) joint aggregate model
    for (basis in c("focal", "aggregate")) {
      preds <- if (basis == "focal") paste0(FOCAL_ANTIGEN, "_", EFFECTORS) else paste0("agg_", EFFECTORS)
      jrows <- list()
      for (m in cfg$maternal_arms) {
        d <- strat[[m]]; pp <- intersect(preds, names(d))
        dd <- d[stats::complete.cases(d[, c(OUTCOME, pp)]), , drop = FALSE]
        if (length(pp) < 2 || nrow(dd) < NETWORK_MIN) next
        f <- lm(reformulate(pp, response = OUTCOME), data = dd); co <- summary(f)$coefficients
        for (p in pp) jrows[[length(jrows)+1]] <- data.frame(
          arm = m, n = nrow(dd), R2_model = round(summary(f)$r.squared, 3),
          effector = sub(paste0("^", FOCAL_ANTIGEN, "_|^agg_"), "", p),
          beta = round(co[p,1],3), p = signif(co[p,4],3), check.names = FALSE)
      }
      jb <- do.call(rbind, jrows)
      if (!is.null(jb)) print(knitr::kable(jb, row.names = FALSE,
        caption = paste0("Joint ", basis, " ADCD+ADCP+ADNP model for ", cfg$outcome_label,
        " [", mode, "]: which effector is independently associated."))); cat("\n")
    }
    .info("step13 [%s] done", mode)
  }
}
```

**How to read this.** If the aggregate score outperforms focal `r FOCAL_ANTIGEN`, the effector's link
to `r cfg$outcome_label` is broad across antigens rather than pertactin-specific. In the joint models,
a single effector retaining a significant coefficient identifies the function that is independently on
the path; if all three collapse (as ADCD did against FcγR3b), the effector functions are collinear
correlates of receptor engagement rather than separable steps. The quantity-removed modes are decisive.
